evals.name: tulip-progressive-development
evals.prompt: 加爾文主義 TULIP 是聖經的教導嗎？
evals.expected_output: Should mention TULIP acronym is a later English mnemonic (~20th c.), five points from Dort 1618-1619, Calvin never articulated these exact 5 points, present progressive development timeline

assertions.text: Mentions TULIP acronym is a later creation, not from Dort itself
Mentions Calvin died 1564 before Dort
Presents Romans 9 as national/covenantal, not individual predestination
Does not present Dort as originating Calvinism (progressive development)

evals.name: john-10-30-observation-vs-interpretation
evals.prompt: 約翰福音 10:30 「我與父原為一」的希臘原文分析
evals.expected_output: Should state hen is neuter (grammatical fact) and present multiple scholarly interpretations (shared purpose vs shared nature) without asserting one as settled by grammar

assertions.text: States hen is neuter, not masculine heis (grammatical fact)
Presents at least 2 scholarly interpretations of hen
Does NOT gloss hen as ONLY 'unity of purpose' without noting debate
Clearly separates grammatical observation from theological interpretation

evals.name: hebrews-8-13-kainos-semantic-range
evals.prompt: 希伯來書 8:13 是說舊約被廢棄嗎？
evals.expected_output: Should present kainos semantic range from BDAG, note that grammar alone doesn't settle renewal vs replacement debate, show Jeremiah 31 context

assertions.text: Distinguishes kainos (qualitative) from neos (temporal)
Cites standard lexicon (BDAG, Thayer, or similar) for kainos definition
Does NOT assert kainos definitively means 'renewed' without noting the range
References Jeremiah 31:31-33 and Torah internalization

evals.name: psalm-46-10-harpu-semantic-range
evals.prompt: 詩篇 46:10 「你們要休息」的希伯來文原意是什麼？
evals.expected_output: Should present raphah root meaning range, note military context of Psalm 46, but not overstate as exclusively 'military command'

assertions.text: Identifies harpu from root raphah with Hifil imperative form
Notes Psalm 46 military context (wars, kingdoms, weapons)
Does NOT call it exclusively a 'military command' (presents semantic range)
Contrasts with common devotional reading while noting the range

evals.name: almah-isaiah-7-14-semantic-range
evals.prompt: 以賽亞書 7:14 的 almah 是處女的意思嗎？
evals.expected_output: Should present almah semantic range (young woman of marriageable age), contrast with betulah, note LXX parthenos translation choice, present scholarly debate without overreaching

assertions.text: Defines almah as young woman of marriageable age (not exclusively virgin)
Contrasts with betulah (more specifically 'virgin')
Mentions LXX translation to parthenos and its significance
Presents the broader context of Isaiah 7:10-17 (Ahaz situation)
Flags RCUV '童女' as translation bias vs 呂振中 '少婦'
Contains [✓ verified] markers on factual claims
Includes 驗證來源 table at end of response

evals.name: church-language-detection-trinity
evals.prompt: 我想了解三位一體的聖經根據
evals.expected_output: Should detect '三位一體' as church language (post-first-century concept), redirect via AskUserQuestion with options like '第一世紀的原始概念' / '概念發展史' / '希伯來文原意', NOT immediately launch into a Trinity defense or attack

assertions.text: Detects 三位一體 as a post-first-century concept and flags it
Calls AskUserQuestion (or text fallback) BEFORE starting interpretation
Offers redirection options (第一世紀的原始概念, 概念發展史, or similar)
Does NOT immediately present a pro-Trinity or anti-Trinity argument without asking first

evals.name: denomination-routing-charismatic
evals.prompt: 我們教會牧師說要用方言禱告才有聖靈充滿，這是聖經的教導嗎？
evals.expected_output: Should detect this as charismatic/Pentecostal denomination question, consult references/denomination-claims.md, present first-century glossolalia evidence, distinguish from modern tongues practice

assertions.text: Identifies this as a charismatic/Pentecostal teaching about tongues
Fetches 1 Corinthians 12-14 and/or Acts 2 scripture text
Distinguishes first-century glossolalia (known languages in Acts 2) from modern tongues practice
References the historical origin of tongues-as-evidence doctrine (Azusa Street 1906 or Pentecostal movement)
Presents evidence without saying 'your pastor is wrong' (guides, doesn't attack)

evals.name: interfaith-buddhism-comparison
evals.prompt: 佛教的慈悲跟聖經的愛有什麼不同？
evals.expected_output: Should analyze Hebrew hesed/ahavah and Greek agape from first-century Jewish perspective, compare with Buddhist karuna/metta conceptual framework, avoid superficial 'they're basically the same' or dismissive 'Buddhism is wrong'

assertions.text: Analyzes Hebrew hesed (covenantal loyalty) and/or ahavah, not just generic 'love'
Explains Buddhist karuna/metta as a distinct conceptual framework, not a Bible equivalent
Highlights the covenantal/relational nature of biblical love vs. the universal/non-attached nature of Buddhist compassion
Does NOT dismiss Buddhism or claim biblical love is 'superior' — presents structural differences

evals.name: ccv-unavailable-ot-fallback
evals.prompt: 請用新漢語譯本分析以賽亞書 40:31
evals.expected_output: Should attempt fetch_ccv.py for Isaiah 40, handle the unavailability gracefully (CCV OT only has Genesis-Joshua + Ruth + Jonah), fall back to other translations, and inform the user

assertions.text: Attempts to fetch CCV for Isaiah and handles the unavailability
Informs user that CCV OT coverage is partial (Isaiah not available)
Falls back to alternative translations (RCUV, 呂振中, or others)
Still provides Hebrew analysis of Isaiah 40:31 (qavah = wait/hope with tension)

evals.name: followup-did-you-know-cycle
evals.prompt: 羅馬書 3:23 的原文分析
evals.expected_output: After presenting the analysis, should end with AskUserQuestion offering 4 options including a 'Did You Know?' fun fact from random_fact.py (excluding Romans). The fact should be from a DIFFERENT book than Romans.

assertions.text: Presents Romans 3:23 analysis with Greek/Hebrew terms (hamartia, Paul's argument about Jew-Gentile equality)
Does NOT frame Romans 3:23 through 'total depravity' or 'original sin' lens
Ends with AskUserQuestion or text fallback with 4 options
Fourth option is 'Did You Know?' with a fact NOT about Romans

evals.name: dss-community-rule-query
evals.prompt: 死海古卷的社群規章 (1QS) 跟耶穌的教導有什麼關係？
evals.expected_output: Should fetch 1QS via fetch_dss.py, present key parallels between Qumran community practices and Yeshua's teachings (purity, communal meals, eschatology), note differences (Qumran exclusivism vs. Yeshua's inclusivism)

assertions.text: Fetches text from fetch_dss.py using 1QS or 社群規章
Identifies parallels between Qumran and Yeshua's teachings (e.g., communal meals, purity, eschatological urgency)
Notes key differences (Qumran's exclusivism and separatism vs. Yeshua's engagement with sinners)
Does NOT claim Yeshua was an Essene or that he copied from Qumran

evals.name: lxx-isaiah-7-14-comparison
evals.prompt: 請比較以賽亞書 7:14 的希伯來原文和七十士譯本的差異
evals.expected_output: Should fetch Hebrew via Sefaria and Greek via fetch_lxx.py. Compare almah (Hebrew) vs. parthenos (LXX Greek). Discuss the translators' choice and its theological implications.

assertions.text: Fetches Hebrew text from Sefaria for Isaiah 7:14
Fetches LXX Greek from fetch_lxx.py for Isaiah 7:14
Identifies almah→parthenos as the key translation shift
Explains that LXX translators chose parthenos (which CAN mean virgin but also young woman) from almah (which does NOT specifically mean virgin)
Notes this LXX choice influenced Matthew 1:23 and Chinese 童女 translation

evals.name: rabbinic-hillel-golden-rule
evals.prompt: 希勒爾的金律跟耶穌的金律有什麼關係？
evals.expected_output: Should fetch Talmud Shabbat 31a via fetch_rabbinic.py. Compare Hillel's negative formulation with Yeshua's positive formulation (Matt 7:12). Show this as intra-Jewish dialogue, not Christianity vs. Judaism.

assertions.text: Fetches Talmud Bavli Shabbat 31a via fetch_rabbinic.py
Presents Hillel's negative formulation: what is hateful to you, do not do to your neighbor
Compares with Yeshua's positive formulation (Matt 7:12) as a development within Jewish tradition
Frames this as intra-Jewish dialogue, not Christianity correcting Judaism
References Leviticus 19:18 as the shared Torah foundation for both

evals.name: translation-comparison-mode
evals.prompt: 比較不同中文譯本對詩篇 23:1 的翻譯
evals.expected_output: Should use mode 4 (譯本比較). Fetch RCUV, CCV (or note unavailability), 呂振中 via FHL, and Sigao. Compare each with Hebrew original. Flag any biases.

assertions.text: Fetches at least 3 different Chinese translations (RCUV, 呂振中, and one more)
Shows Hebrew original text with transliteration
Compares how each translation renders key terms (YHWH as shepherd, ro'i)
Notes translation differences or biases where present
Auto-detects intent (translation comparison) without needing to ask via AskUserQuestion

evals.name: topical-study-hesed
evals.prompt: 希伯來文 hesed 的主題研究
evals.expected_output: Should use mode 3 (主題研究). Present hesed's full semantic range, key passages across Tanakh, first-century covenantal meaning, contrast with common mistranslations.

assertions.text: Defines hesed as covenantal loyalty (not generic mercy/loving-kindness)
Cites multiple Tanakh passages where hesed appears (e.g., Exodus 34:6, Hosea, Psalms, Ruth)
References hebrew-key-terms.md verified entry for hesed
Distinguishes hesed from Greek agape and from modern Chinese translations
Presents the covenantal (berit) framework as essential context

evals.name: pseudepigrapha-1-enoch-son-of-man
evals.prompt: 以諾一書的人子跟福音書的人子有什麼關係？
evals.expected_output: Should fetch 1 Enoch 46-48 via fetch_pseudepigrapha.py. Compare the Enochic Son of Man (Parables of Enoch) with Yeshua's self-designation and Daniel 7:13 bar enash.

assertions.text: Fetches 1 Enoch text (chapters 46-48 or nearby) via fetch_pseudepigrapha.py
Identifies the Son of Man figure in the Parables of Enoch (1 Enoch 37-71)
Connects to Daniel 7:13 bar enash as the shared source tradition
Notes scholarly debate about dating of the Parables (no Parables fragments found at Qumran)
Does NOT collapse the Enochic and Gospel Son of Man into identical figures — shows development within Second Temple Judaism

evals.name: trinitarian-formula-hebrew-matthew-vulgate
evals.prompt: 教父怎麼引用馬太福音 28:19？希伯來馬太福音有什麼不同？
evals.expected_output: Should fetch Matt 28:19-20 from multiple sources: standard Greek (FHL/BibleGateway), Hebrew Matthew (fetch_hebrew_matthew.py shem-tov and du-tillet), Latin Vulgate (fetch_vulgate.py), and Apostolic Fathers for early citations (fetch_apostolic_fathers.py for Didache 7). Compare the Trinitarian baptismal formula across witnesses.

assertions.text: Fetches Hebrew Matthew via fetch_hebrew_matthew.py (at least shem-tov)
Notes Shem-Tov Hebrew Matthew lacks the Trinitarian baptismal formula at 28:19-20
Fetches Latin Vulgate via fetch_vulgate.py for Matt 28
Fetches or references Didache chapter 7 via fetch_apostolic_fathers.py as early baptismal evidence
Presents the textual evidence without asserting which reading is 'original' — notes the manuscript situation

evals.name: aramaic-talitha-kumi
evals.prompt: 馬可福音 5:41 的 Talitha kumi 是什麼意思？為什麼馬可保留了亞蘭文原話？
evals.expected_output: Should fetch Mark 5:41 text, identify Talitha qumi as Aramaic, explain meaning (little girl, arise), discuss why the Gospel preserves original Aramaic words, reference aramaic-key-terms.md.

assertions.text: Fetches Mark 5:41 scripture text via fetch_fhl.py or fetch_biblegateway.py
Identifies Talitha qumi as Aramaic (not Hebrew or Greek)
Explains meaning: talitha = little girl/lamb, qumi = arise (feminine imperative)
References aramaic-key-terms.md for verified term data
Discusses why Gospel preserves original Aramaic (eyewitness memory, not magic formula)
Does NOT frame preserved Aramaic words as incantations or spells

evals.name: church-practice-altar-call
evals.prompt: 祭壇呼召和決志禱告是聖經的做法嗎？
evals.expected_output: Should Grep church-practices.md for altar call and sinner's prayer entries, present historical origins (Finney 1820s, Albert Gage 1922), contrast with first-century practice, reference scripture-to-denomination.md if relevant.

assertions.text: Looks up altar call in church-practices.md (Finney, 1820s-1830s)
Looks up sinner's prayer in church-practices.md (Albert Gage, 1922)
Presents historical origins with dates and key figures
Contrasts with first-century Jewish practice (no equivalent in Second Temple Judaism)
Does NOT attack these practices as 'wrong' — presents historical context and lets user decide

evals.name: yeshua-hermeneutics-kal-vachomer
evals.prompt: 耶穌用了什麼拉比解經方法？舉例說明 kal va-chomer
evals.expected_output: Should reference yeshua-hermeneutics.md for the 27 interpretive methods. Present kal va-chomer (light to heavy / a fortiori) with specific Gospel examples. Show Yeshua working within Jewish interpretive tradition.

assertions.text: References yeshua-hermeneutics.md for interpretive methods
Explains kal va-chomer (קל וחומר) as 'light to heavy' / a fortiori argument
Gives at least one specific Gospel example of Yeshua using kal va-chomer (e.g., Matt 6:26-30 birds/lilies, Matt 7:11 earthly fathers/heavenly Father, Matt 12:11-12 sheep/human on Sabbath)
Mentions this is one of the standard rabbinic middot (rules of interpretation)
Frames Yeshua as participating in Jewish interpretive tradition, not inventing something new
